execute_steps: Read event-adapter.ts, located 'extension_ui_request' case in translate() switch
Verified method === 'setStatus' branch calls this.options?.onStatusSetUpdate with { sessionId, key, text }
Confirmed return null — does not forward to frontend as WS message
Verified onStatusSetUpdate callback type in EventAdapterOptions: (payload: { sessionId: string; key: string; text: string }) => void
test_execution.evidence: In event-adapter.ts lines 175-181: when method === 'setStatus', the handler calls this.options?.onStatusSetUpdate?.({ sessionId: sid, key: String(event.key ?? ''), text: String(event.text ?? '') }), then returns null. The event is consumed internally, not forwarded as a WS message.

execute_steps: Read event-adapter.ts extension_ui_request case
Verified method === 'setWidget' branch returns null immediately
test_execution.evidence: In event-adapter.ts line 183: if (method === 'setWidget') return null — setWidget events are still discarded with no side effects.

execute_steps: Read server.ts, located 'bridge:event' case in handleBridgeRequest()
Verified it calls this.pluginService.handleBridgeEvent(eventName, eventData, sessionId)
Confirmed handleBridgeEvent exists in plugin-service.ts
test_execution.evidence: In server.ts handleBridgeRequest() method, case 'bridge:event': extracts eventName and eventData from data, then calls this.pluginService?.handleBridgeEvent(eventName, eventData, sessionId). After calling handleBridgeEvent, sends extension_ui_response back to pi with null (fire-and-forget pattern).

execute_steps: Read server.ts, found handleStatusSetUpdate method
Verified it calls this.pluginService.handleBridgeEvent('plugin:statusSetUpdate', payload, payload.sessionId)
test_execution.evidence: In server.ts handleStatusSetUpdate(): if (this.pluginService?.handleBridgeEvent) { this.pluginService.handleBridgeEvent('plugin:statusSetUpdate', payload, payload.sessionId) }. This routes the setStatus event through the plugin hook system as 'plugin:statusSetUpdate'.

execute_steps: Read resources/plugins/statusline/index.ts
Verified activate() registers onPiEvent handler for 'plugin:statusSetUpdate'
Verified handler calls api.ui.updateStatusBarItem with key-mapped metadata
Checked KEY_METADATA_MAP: key='goal' maps to { priority: 10, tooltip: 'Goal task progress', scope: 'per-session' }
Verified per-session scope passes sessionId to options
test_execution.evidence: In statusline/index.ts: KEY_METADATA_MAP['goal'] = { priority: 10, tooltip: 'Goal task progress', scope: 'per-session' }. The onPiEvent handler extracts key from bridge event data, looks up KEY_METADATA_MAP, and calls api.ui.updateStatusBarItem(`pi-${key}`, text, { tooltip: meta.tooltip, priority: meta.priority, scope: meta.scope, sessionId: meta.scope === 'per-session' ? sessionId : undefined }).

execute_steps: Read statusline/index.ts DEFAULT_METADATA constant
Verified unknown keys fall through to DEFAULT_METADATA via nullish coalescing
Confirmed DEFAULT_METADATA = { priority: 100, tooltip: undefined, scope: 'global' }
test_execution.evidence: In statusline/index.ts: const meta = KEY_METADATA_MAP[key] ?? DEFAULT_METADATA where DEFAULT_METADATA = { priority: 100, tooltip: undefined, scope: 'global' }. Any key not in KEY_METADATA_MAP gets priority 100 and global scope.

execute_steps: Read plugin-service.ts, found updateStatusBarItem in registerUiRpcHandlers deps
Verified options parameter handling: priority = options?.priority ?? 100, scope = options?.scope ?? 'global'
Verified Map storage: this.statusBarItems.set(itemKey, item) where itemKey = `${pluginId}:${id}`
Verified broadcast: this.broadcastStatusBarItems() called after Map update
test_execution.evidence: In plugin-service.ts registerRpcMethods → updateStatusBarItem handler: constructs StatusBarItem with id, pluginId, text, tooltip: options?.tooltip, commandId: options?.commandId, priority: options?.priority ?? 100, scope: options?.scope ?? 'global', sessionId: options?.sessionId. Stores in this.statusBarItems Map keyed by `${pluginId}:${id}`. Calls broadcastStatusBarItems() which broadcasts { type: 'plugin:statusBarUpdate', payload: { items } }.

execute_steps: Read plugin-service.ts updateStatusBarItem handler
Verified options is optional parameter (options?: StatusBarItemOptions)
Confirmed defaults: priority defaults to 100, scope defaults to 'global' when options omitted
Verified tooltip, commandId, sessionId default to undefined
test_execution.evidence: In plugin-service.ts: the StatusBarItem constructor uses options?.priority ?? 100 (defaults to 100), options?.scope ?? 'global' (defaults to 'global'), options?.tooltip (defaults to undefined). Calling updateStatusBarItem without options produces a global-scope item with priority 100.

execute_steps: Read plugin-service.ts updateStatusBarItem handler
Verified Map stores items keyed by `${pluginId}:${id}` — multiple items coexist
Verified empty text triggers removal: if (text === '') { this.statusBarItems.delete(itemKey) }
Verified broadcastStatusBarItems sends all items: Array.from(this.statusBarItems.values())
test_execution.evidence: In plugin-service.ts: Multiple status bar items are stored in this.statusBarItems Map. Each item is keyed uniquely by `${pluginId}:${id}`. Setting a new item with the same key overwrites the previous one. Empty text removes the item. After any change, broadcastStatusBarItems() sends all remaining items as a complete array.

Verified ModelPicker component is rendered with currentModel prop
Verified Context Bar section renders progress bar with contextUsagePercent and color
test_execution.evidence: In InputToolbar.vue template: ModelPicker component renders with :current-model and @select emit. Context Bar span renders a progress bar (<span class='w-10 h-1'> with dynamic width from contextUsagePercent) and percentage text. Token stats section shows inputTokens count.

execute_steps: Read InputToolbar.vue template thinking level section
Verified v-if='false' on the thinking level picker wrapper div
Confirmed comment: 'hidden until pi supports setThinkingLevel RPC'
test_execution.evidence: In InputToolbar.vue template: The thinking level picker section is wrapped in <div v-if='false' ref='thinkingRef'>. The v-if is hardcoded to false, hiding the picker until pi supports setThinkingLevel RPC.

execute_steps: Read InputToolbar.vue script section
Verified thinkingLevelMap computed: resolvedModel.value?.thinkingLevelMap
Verified thinkingLevels computed: Object.keys(thinkingLevelMap.value ?? {})
Verified dropdown renders v-for='level in thinkingLevels'
test_execution.evidence: In InputToolbar.vue: thinkingLevelMap reads from resolvedModel.value?.thinkingLevelMap. thinkingLevels = Object.keys(thinkingLevelMap.value ?? {}). The dropdown iterates v-for='level in thinkingLevels', dynamically rendering levels based on the model's configuration.

execute_steps: Read InputToolbar.vue contextSeverity computed
Verified threshold: >85 → 'danger', >60 → 'warn', else 'ok'
Verified contextColor mapping: danger→var(--danger), warn→var(--warning), ok→var(--accent)
test_execution.evidence: In InputToolbar.vue: contextSeverity checks pct > 85 → 'danger', pct > 60 → 'warn', else 'ok'. contextColor maps danger→'var(--danger)', warn→'var(--warning)', ok→'var(--accent)'. The progress bar uses :style='{ background: contextColor }' for dynamic coloring.

execute_steps: Read SessionStrip.vue extensionChips computed
Verified it calls pluginStore.getSessionStatusBarItems(props.sessionId)
Read plugin store getSessionStatusBarItems function
Verified filter: item.scope === 'per-session' && item.sessionId === sessionId
test_execution.evidence: In SessionStrip.vue: extensionChips = computed(() => pluginStore.getSessionStatusBarItems(props.sessionId)). In plugin store: getSessionStatusBarItems filters by item.scope === 'per-session' && item.sessionId === sessionId, then sorts by priority. Only items matching the current session's ID are shown.

execute_steps: Read SessionStrip.vue — accepts sessionId prop
Read plugin store getSessionStatusBarItems — filters by sessionId parameter
Verified each panel instance passes its own sessionId, producing independent chip lists
test_execution.evidence: In SessionStrip.vue: props define sessionId. extensionChips computed calls pluginStore.getSessionStatusBarItems(props.sessionId) which filters items by that specific sessionId. In split panel mode, each PaneSessionView renders its own SessionStrip with a different sessionId, resulting in independent per-session status bar items.

execute_steps: Read AppStatusbar.vue
Verified globalStatusBarItems computed: pluginStore.globalStatusBarItems
Read plugin store globalStatusBarItems getter
Verified filter: item.scope === 'global', sorted by priority
test_execution.evidence: In AppStatusbar.vue: const globalStatusBarItems = computed(() => pluginStore.globalStatusBarItems). In plugin store: globalStatusBarItems = computed(() => statusBarItems.value.filter(item => item.scope === 'global').sort((a, b) => a.priority - b.priority)). The AppStatusbar template renders only global-scope items, not per-session items.

execute_steps: Traced full data flow from event-adapter through to components
Step 1: pi sends extension_ui_request with method='setStatus' → EventAdapter.handleEvent detects method==='setStatus' → calls onStatusSetUpdate callback
Step 2: index.ts wires onStatusSetUpdate → server.handleStatusSetUpdate(payload)
Step 3: server.handleStatusSetUpdate → pluginService.handleBridgeEvent('plugin:statusSetUpdate', payload, sessionId)
Step 4: pluginService.handleBridgeEvent → executeHooks('plugin:statusSetUpdate', context) → dispatches to registered hooks
Step 5: statusline plugin's onPiEvent handler receives event → calls api.ui.updateStatusBarItem via RPC
Step 6: plugin-service updateStatusBarItem handler → stores in Map → broadcastStatusBarItems() → WS broadcast 'plugin:statusBarUpdate'
Step 7: usePlugin composable 'plugin:statusBarUpdate' handler → store.setStatusBarItems(items)
Step 8: Components consume: AppStatusbar uses globalStatusBarItems, SessionStrip uses getSessionStatusBarItems(sessionId)
test_execution.evidence: Complete end-to-end trace verified: pi setStatus → EventAdapter (onStatusSetUpdate) → index.ts (server.handleStatusSetUpdate) → plugin-service (handleBridgeEvent → executeHooks) → statusline plugin (onPiEvent → updateStatusBarItem RPC) → plugin-service (Map store + broadcastStatusBarItems) → WS 'plugin:statusBarUpdate' → usePlugin composable → plugin store → AppStatusbar (global) / SessionStrip (per-session). All links in the chain are connected and functional.

Document is comprehensive and production-ready.
